description: Hard, trap-laden, DETERMINISTIC-answer reasoning problems designed to DISCRIMINATE reasoning models from cheap models. Each has a unique correct answer and a tempting plausible-but-wrong shortcut; scoring is purely deterministic (quality = did it reach the right answer), so a model cannot bluff a judge — reasoning shows up objectively. Spans multi-step arithmetic, logic deduction, constraint satisfaction, dependency ordering, code reasoning, probability, date/rate reasoning, and formal logic. Cheap models should trip the traps; reasoning models should work them through — revealing where reasoning earns its cost.

tasks.title: Sequential percentages (net-zero trap)
tasks.prompt: A product costs $80. The price is first increased by 25%, then a 25% discount is applied to that new price, then 8% sales tax is added. What is the final price? Return ONLY the final number rounded to 2 decimals (just the number, no $).

tasks.title: Knights and knaves deduction
tasks.prompt: On an island, knights always tell the truth and knaves always lie. A says: 'B is a knave.' B says: 'A and I are the same type.' Determine each one's type. Return ONLY JSON: {"A": "knight"|"knave", "B": "knight"|"knave"}.

tasks.title: Valid dependency (topological) ordering
tasks.prompt: Tasks and their prerequisites: A has none; B needs A; C needs A; D needs B and C; E needs D. Give one valid execution order of all five. Return ONLY a comma-separated list of the letters (e.g. 'A, B, C, D, E').

tasks.title: Subtle off-by-one in a prime check
Expected value (forgets the loss outcomes)
tasks.prompt: A game: you roll one fair 6-sided die. If it shows a 6 you win $30; on any other result you lose $5. What is the expected value of one play, in dollars? Return ONLY the number rounded to 2 decimals (a negative sign if it's a loss).

tasks.title: Rate problem (the classic scaling trap)
tasks.prompt: If 3 machines make 3 widgets in 3 minutes, how many minutes do 100 machines take to make 100 widgets (same rate per machine)? Return ONLY JSON: {"minutes": <number>}.

tasks.title: Proportional distance (unit care)
tasks.prompt: A car travels 60 km in 45 minutes at constant speed. At that same speed, how many km does it travel in 1 hour and 15 minutes? Return ONLY the number of km.

tasks.title: Business-day date reasoning
tasks.prompt: A task starts on Monday, June 1, and takes 7 business days to complete (work happens Monday-Friday only; weekends don't count). On what calendar date is the 7th business day? Return ONLY the date in the form 'June D' (e.g. 'June 9').

tasks.title: Conditional logic (denying the consequent)
tasks.prompt: It is TRUE that: 'If it rains, the game is cancelled.' You are told the game was NOT cancelled. What can you validly conclude about whether it rained? Return ONLY JSON: {"conclusion": "it did not rain"|"it rained"|"cannot tell"}.

tasks.title: Constraint satisfaction (unique assignment)
tasks.prompt: Ann, Bob, and Cal each own exactly one distinct pet from {cat, dog, fish}. Clues: Ann does not own the dog. Bob owns neither the cat nor the dog. Determine who owns what. Return ONLY JSON: {"Ann": "cat"|"dog"|"fish", "Bob": ..., "Cal": ...}.

tasks.title: Number sequence (non-constant difference)
tasks.prompt: What is the next number in the sequence: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ? Return ONLY the number.

tasks.title: Set reasoning (inclusion-exclusion)
tasks.prompt: In a group of 30 people, 18 like tea, 15 like coffee, and 5 like neither. How many like BOTH tea and coffee? Return ONLY the number.
